currentUse: The former St. George campus at 545 Hudson Avenue houses House of Prayer and Restoration (HOPAR), a Spanish-speaking Apostolic/Pentecostal congregation; the St. George Lithuanian community itself worships at Our Lady of Lourdes Church, 150 Varinna Drive, Brighton (Rochester), NY, within the Our Lady of Lourdes & St. Anne cluster.
summary: St. George Lithuanian parish left its historic Hudson Avenue campus voluntarily: the community celebrated its final Mass at 545 Hudson Avenue on June 20, 2010, and moved to Our Lady of Lourdes Parish in Brighton, where its first Mass was held June 27, 2010. The 1934/1935 church and the 1910 three-story hall still stand and were sold by the parish to a Puerto Rican congregation; the buildings today house House of Prayer and Restoration (HOPAR), an Apostolic church led by pastors Jose and Sonia Diaz, with Sunday worship at 10:00 a.m. Stained-glass windows with Lithuanian inscriptions remain in the building, while the Lithuanians removed the entrance stone and the Our Lady of Šiluva and Our Lady of Vilnius murals before the transfer, and artifacts from the parish's Lithuanian Ethnic Museum (founded 1976) were sent to Lithuania. The Lithuanian community survives as the "Ročesterio Šv. Jurgio lietuvių katalikų misijinė bendruomenė" (St. George Lithuanian Catholic mission community), listed in Lithuanian pastoral-care directories as worshiping at Our Lady of Lourdes; the Diocese of Rochester's directory still carries St. George (Lithuanian) as part of the Our Lady of Lourdes & Saint Anne cluster, listing a Sunday 12:30 p.m. Lithuanian Mass with schedule marked "TBD." The host diocese emerged from a six-year Chapter 11 bankruptcy when a $246.35 million clergy-abuse settlement was approved on September 5, 2025 — relevant restructuring context for all Rochester parishes, though no St. George-specific impact was found.

developments.headline: Final Mass on Hudson Avenue; community relocates to Our Lady of Lourdes
developments.detail: After more than a century on Hudson Avenue, St. George Lithuanian parish celebrated its final Mass at 545 Hudson Avenue on June 20, 2010, with Father Dominic Mockevicius as pastor, and moved to Our Lady of Lourdes Parish in Brighton, with its first Mass there at noon on June 27, 2010. The parish had been incorporated in 1908; its original combined church-school-hall building was dedicated in 1910, the rectory built in 1925, and a second church in 1935.

sources.title: St. George Lithuanian celebrates final Mass on Hudson Avenue

developments.headline: Hudson Avenue campus becomes a Hispanic Apostolic church; Lithuanian heritage items dispersed
developments.detail: The church and hall were sold by the parish to a Puerto Rican congregation and now house House of Prayer and Restoration (HOPAR), an Apostolic church pastored by Jose and Sonia Diaz (Sunday worship 10:00 a.m.). Stained-glass windows with Lithuanian inscriptions remain inside; the Lithuanians removed the entrance stone and the murals of Our Lady of Šiluva and Our Lady of Vilnius, and artifacts of the 1976-founded Lithuanian Ethnic Museum were sent to Lithuania, with some items and a traditional Lithuanian cross relocated to Our Lady of Lourdes.

sources.title: Upstate New York: Lithuanians and Lithuanian heritage
House of Prayer and Restoration - Rochester, NY

developments.headline: St. George (Lithuanian) still listed in diocesan directory within the Our Lady of Lourdes & St. Anne cluster
developments.detail: The Diocese of Rochester's Pastoral Services Directory lists St. George (Lithuanian) at 150 Varinna Drive, Rochester NY 14618, part of the Our Lady of Lourdes & Saint Anne cluster (pastor listed as Rev. Gary Tyman), with a Sunday 12:30 p.m. Lithuanian Mass at Our Lady of Lourdes marked 'TBD' and a dedicated parish office phone. The Lithuanian pastoral-care directory sielovada.org likewise lists the 'Ročesterio Šv. Jurgio lietuvių katalikų misijinė bendruomenė' as celebrating Mass at Our Lady of Lourdes.

sources.title: Šiaurės Amerika
sources.publisher: sielovada.org (Lithuanian Bishops' Conference delegate for foreign Lithuanian Catholics)

developments.headline: Host diocese exits Chapter 11: $246.35M abuse settlement approved
developments.detail: U.S. Bankruptcy Judge Paul Warren approved the Diocese of Rochester's $246.35 million settlement with more than 500 clergy-abuse claimants, ending a Chapter 11 case filed in September 2019; Continental Insurance (CNA) pays $120 million, with the diocese, its parishes, and other insurers covering the rest. This is the restructuring backdrop for the cluster hosting the St. George community; no St. George-specific impact was reported.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions — the findings corroborate the archive's core claim: the diocese did not close the parish; the community left Hudson Avenue on its own, sold its buildings, and the parish/mission community formally continues with Lithuanian Mass. Refinements: (1) the departure from Hudson Avenue is precisely dated — final Mass June 20, 2010, first Mass at Our Lady of Lourdes June 27, 2010; (2) the archive says the community 'moved to rented space,' but sources describe it as worshiping within a host Catholic parish (Our Lady of Lourdes & St. Anne cluster) — no source characterized the arrangement as a rental, so 'hosted within another parish' may be the more accurate framing; (3) the archive's 'monthly Lithuanian Mass' frequency could not be confirmed — the diocesan directory lists a Sunday 12:30 p.m. Lithuanian Mass with schedule marked 'TBD,' so current frequency is unverified rather than contradicted; (4) the archive says 'Closed: not stated' — 2010 can be recorded as the year the community left its own buildings, though the parish/mission itself is not closed.
gaps: Sale details for the Hudson Avenue campus (buyer entity, deed year, price) were not found in press coverage — Global True Lithuania says only 'sold to Puerto Ricans in the 2000s'; Monroe County Real Property Portal would need a direct records lookup. Current Lithuanian Mass frequency and celebrant are unverified: the diocesan directory shows Sunday 12:30 p.m. 'TBD' and the cluster website (ololsa.org) returned HTTP 403; the pastor listing (Rev. Gary Tyman) may be outdated. Whether the parish remains canonically distinct or was formally merged into the Our Lady of Lourdes/St. Anne parish is not stated in any opened source. No preservation campaign, reunion, or recent (2024-2026) press specifically about St. George was found; a Catholic Courier centennial article exists but appears to date from the 2008 anniversary, not recent activity. No St. George-specific exposure in the diocesan bankruptcy was found.
